THE NEXT WORK, named by Akien — the DIAG.trigger: Along the way, system_rackmount's proof was found FLAKY: red 2/5 under a tight suite loop, GREEN 15/15 in isolation. It is Postgres-backed (the real bus rides db_domain). CC did NOT touch system_rackmount. SUSPICIOUS: the red runs printed NOTHING — no assertion message, no trace — a SILENT red, itself a suspected Law 7 breach (an error not loud at the diagnostic surface). Root cause is a HYPOTHESIS (DB/connection contention under load), NOT measured.
THE NEXT WORK, named by Akien — the DIAG.the_method_Akien_ruled: EXACT ORDER (Akien's precise restatement): LOGGING -> a LOG INTERPRETER -> REVIEW -> FIX. (1) LOGGING — instrument the chain: EVERY value at EVERY state transition and EVERY boundary crossing logged, at the 6TH PLACE AFTER THE DECIMAL (a distinct fine-grained level/precision) so every entry is capturable and NONE are lost. (2) A LOG INTERPRETER — build the processor that COMPILES THE INFERENCE over those logs (Law 1 on the diagnostic surface, the same move as inference_domain) so CC can point the magic wand and get a DIAGNOSTIC SLICE for anywhere along the workflow. It comes BEFORE review because the review is done by READING its output — the interpreter is the instrument, not a downstream tool. (3) REVIEW — read the slice ourselves; escalate to /advisor or Fable ONLY if the evidence still doesn't resolve it (the conditional step, not the reflex). (4) FIX. CC had it backwards once (proposed Fable-to-diagnose as step 1; CC--). Recorded in memory: diagnostic-logging-method.md.
THE NEXT WORK, named by Akien — the DIAG.the_interpreter_is_a_real_build: The log interpreter is a REAL new build (a spine / thing-for-akien tool), not a fix step — inference_domain's move aimed at the diagnostic surface. Reusable: once it exists, every future unclear issue is instrument -> interpret -> review. No ticket yet; /intent it when the logging work reveals its shape.
THE NEXT WORK, named by Akien — the DIAG.so_the_immediate_plan: system_rackmount FIRST, via the method IN ORDER: (1) instrument the system_rackmount + bus + db_domain chain with full diagnostic logging (6th-decimal level); (2) build the log interpreter that compiles a diagnostic slice; (3) reproduce the flake under load, read the slice, self-review — escalate only if it doesn't resolve; (4) fix.
